subject: French
coverageNote: Form directories exist only where teaching-module source PDFs were ingested. Missing forms must resolve as known-gap, not empty objects.

lesson.title: 13.0 THE PAST
topics: The past refers to a time that has already happened, before the present moment. It
could be recent (yesterday, last week) or distant (years ago). The past usually means
past tense and it is used to talk about actions or events that have already happened. In
French, learners start by learning passé compose which is the most commonly used
activities:  Play an audio or read a passage in which le passé composé has been used for
the learners to understand the structure and its usage.
 Guide learners in identifying the structure (le passé composé) from the
passage e.g. Hier, je suis allé au marché. J’ai acheté une pomme.
 In groups, learners identify the structure (le passé composé) used from the
 Explain the formation of le passé compose.
assessment: learning activities from all the sub-topics taught and the summative
ii. L’auxiliaire (helping verb) – Either avoir or être, used to form the past tense.
